trivia_bullets.text: Expose the Culprit is a modal instant that interacts with the Disguise and Cloak mechanics introduced in MKM. Disguise lets cards be cast face-down as a 2/2 with ward {2}, concealing their identity until turned face up. The 'expose' mode forces a face-down creature face up — the investigative reveal — while the second mode re-cloaks your own disguised creatures, resetting their hidden identities. The two modes together capture the MKM investigation theme: unmask the enemy, re-mask your allies. [Scryfall] [Wizards: MKM Mechanics]

Art by Ryan Valle depicts a grand ballroom scene — a central figure in a green dress with swirling red energy surrounded by a formal crowd including a blue-skinned figure. The composition is a classic unmasking set piece: the central figure turning mid-stride with red magic trailing suggests an involuntary or forced reveal. No flavor text on this card. [Scryfall]

The second mode — exiling your own face-up creatures with disguise in a face-down pile, shuffling, and re-cloaking them — is a unique effect that resets ward {2} protections and re-randomizes which of your creatures is which, creating significant complexity for opponents trying to track. Legal in Standard, Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, Vintage, and Commander. [Scryfall]
